scripture_text: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God. And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you.
Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.
Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Be still, and know that I am God.
And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help. My help cometh from the LORD, which made heaven and earth.
The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.
Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope.
Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God.
Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed.
What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
Be strong and of a good courage, fear not, nor be afraid of them.
Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil.
When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.
The LORD is on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?
I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
The Lord is my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.
I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress: my God; in him will I trust.
Say to them that are of a fearful heart, Be strong, fear not.
There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear.
The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.
And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.
Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
It is of the LORD'S mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not. They are new every morning.
To appoint unto them that mourn in Zion, to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning.
Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God.
For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength.
My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
For the joy of the LORD is your strength.
Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the LORD.
Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.
In the day when I cried thou answeredst me, and strengthenedst me with strength in my soul.
For I the LORD thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
The LORD shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
Wait on the LORD: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart.
Be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord.
If God be for us, who can be against us?
And the LORD, he it is that doth go before thee; he will be with thee.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus.
As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.
Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.
He will turn again, he will have compassion upon us; he will subdue our iniquities.
In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins.
In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins.
I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid.
For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins and their iniquities will I remember no more.
Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out.
Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
I, even I, am he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake.
Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature.
For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath.
Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you.
Let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding.
But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.
Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath.
Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you.
He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty.
Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people.
Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
For if ye forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise.
Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.
In thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
Rejoice evermore. Pray without ceasing. In every thing give thanks.
This is the day which the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above.
The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped.
Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust, and not be afraid.
O come, let us sing unto the LORD: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.
And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus.
These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you.
It is a good thing to give thanks unto the LORD, and to sing praises unto thy name, O most High.
A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.
Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory.
